title: Integrated Applied Modeling and Communication · Cumulative Modeling Board
summary: A cumulative set of unfamiliar household, workplace, civic, measurement, data, and computational decisions with no copied exercise wording. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret a number together with its unit, reference population, time window, and practical meaning.
State assumptions and distinguish measured, given, estimated, and derived values.
Evaluate grouped expressions with a documented order of operations and intermediate checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Quantity Meaning evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Quantity Meaning assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Interpret a number together with its unit, reference population, time window, and practical meaning. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the boundaries of the idea, compare a valid case with a near miss, and trace the causal difference.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math beginner modeling capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Quantity Meaning, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an independently validated applied-math decision packet.

content.text: A bare number has the same meaning in every context. This fails the Quantity Meaning contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Solve the performance case
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 19 units, changes by −6 units, then by +2 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend a recommendation with calculations, visual evidence, alternatives, and clear next actions.
